titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Geraldine Page Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 7186949.
This collection contains 41 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 845 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 28, 2024 at 01:45

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (133 boxes) + 1 cold storage, 1 record album storage, 2 broadside
did.abstract: The papers include personal and professional correspondence, writings, scripts, photographs, legal and financial documents, production programs, personal memorabilia, artwork, audiovisual materials, press clippings, and other printed material documenting Geraldine Page's personal life and career as a stage, film, and television actress.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 78, 87-88, 128 (Audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 89 (Cold storage): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 90 (Record album): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 137 (Computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: November 2005 acquisition: Purchased from Angelica Page on the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund, 2005.
May 2021 acquisition: Purchased from Imaginal Life LLC on the Sinclair Lewis Fund and the Adele Gutman Nathan Theatrical Collection Fund, 2021.
June 2022 acquisition: Purchased from Imaginal Life LLC on the Sinclair Lewis Fund and the Adele Gutman Nathan Theatrical Collection Fund, 2022.
August 2024 acquisition: Purchased from Imaginal Life LLC on the Adele Gutman Nathan Theatrical Collection Fund, 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into four groupings: I. November 2005 acquisition, 1865-1999. II. May 2021 acquisition, 1836-1987, undated. III. June 2022 Acquisition, 1924 - 1986. IV. July 2024 acquisition.

bioghist.head: Biographical/Historical note
bioghist.p: Geraldine Page was born in Kirksville, Missouri in 1924, and grew up in Chicago where she acted in plays with a church youth group. Following high school she studied at the Goodman Theater School for three years, and with other students from the Goodman formed a stock theater company in which she was active for four seasons. After moving to New York in the late 1940s and working at various odd jobs, she was cast in the 1952 off-Broadway production of Tennessee Williams's Summer and Smoke directed by Jose Quintero. She drew wide praise for her performance, and the following year she made her Broadway debut in Mid-Summer by Vina Delmar. She went on to star in productions on and off-Broadway including The Immoralist (1954) , The Rainmaker (1954), Sweet Bird of Youth (1959), Absurd Person Singular (1974), Clothes for a Summer Hotel (1980), Agnes of God (1982), Vivat! Vivat! Regina! (1985), A Lie of the Mind (1985,) and Blithe Spirit (1987). A student of Method acting, she studied at the Actor's Studio, and worked with the Mirror Repertory Company and the Sanctuary Theatre Workshop, Inc.

Page's film work included Hondo (1954), film versions of Summer and Smoke and Sweet Bird of Youth (1962), The Day of the Locust (1975), The Pope of Greenwich Village (1984) and The Trip to Bountiful (1985), for which she won the Academy Award for best actress. On television she appeared in numerous series and television movies, and won Emmy awards for A Christmas Memory (1966) and The Thanksgiving Visitor (1967), both based on stories by Truman Capote.

In addition to the awards mentioned above, Page received numerous nominations for her film and stage work and was the recipient of the Sarah Siddons Award, the British Academy Film Award, and the National Board of Review Award for Best Actress.

Page was married to the violinist Alexander Schneider from 1954 to 1957. She married the actor Rip Torn in 1963, and they had three children, Angelica, Jon and Tony. She died in New York City in 1987.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Geraldine Page Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection is comprised of materials formerly classed as Uncat MSS 817. These materials received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers include personal and professional correspondence, writings, scripts, photographs, legal and financial documents, production programs, personal memorabilia, artwork, audiovisual materials, press clippings, and other printed material documenting Geraldine Page's personal life and career as a stage, film, and television actress.

Correspondence with family members (including her parents and her husband, the actor Rip Torn), friends, and professional colleagues (including Cheryl Crawford, Elia Kazan, Sissy Spacek, and others), as well as fan mail dates from the 1920s through 1987. Scripts for stage, film, and television include annotated scripts for productions in which she performed as well as scripts sent to her and her husband for review, some accompanied by cover letters. Photographic material includes family portraits and candid photographs, head shots, and production photographs from stage and film productions including Summer and Smoke , Sweet Bird of Youth , Agnes of God , The Trip to Bountiful and others. Her work is further documented by press clippings and other printed material.

Records relating to the production of the films Sweet Bird of Youth and Nasty Habits include contracts, casting call sheets, script pages, and photographs. Also included in the papers are records regarding her work with the Actors Studio, the Poetry Center of the 92nd Street Y, The Academy of Television Arts and Sciences, The New Dramatists Committee and other organizations.
The papers also include artwork by Page and others, and personal items including address books, date books, stationery, and articles of clothing.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Geraldine Page Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.physdesc: (88 boxes) + 1 cold storage, 1 record album storage, 1 broadside folder

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 78 (Audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains scripts, production materials, and related materials from theatrical productions involving Geraldine Page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain materials relating to James Dean and the James Dean foundation including correspondence, photographs, printed material, a ribbon, and a copy of the script for The Immortalist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain schedules, call sheets, box office statements and other papers relating to various productions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain production materials including schedules, call sheets, stationary and other papers relating to various productions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains the upper portion of Geradline Page's cast chair from an unidentified production.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains extensive correspondence written or received by Geraldine Page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, ephemera, clippings, and other materials related to theatrical companies that Geraldine Page worked with during the course of her career. Page's husband, the actor Rip Torn, shared many of these affiliations and his name is mentioned in some of the materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain photographs, printed material, correspondence, production materials, and scripts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain photographs, production materials, notes, and printed material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain correspondence, printed material, photographs, and production materials.

scopecontent.p: These files contain slides, tickets, production materials, correspondence, and scripts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs documenting Page's professional and personal life, including publicity stills from multiple theatrical productions.

scopecontent.p: These files contain photographs of Geraldine Page and of productions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The files contain black and white and color photographs of Geraldine Page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains family papers, address books and rolodexes, artwork, and other papers related to Page's personal life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain artwork by Geraldine Page and James Dean and notes by Geraldine Page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains two lithographs of Geraldine Page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains documents relating to the Page family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains printed materials collected by Geraldine Page.

accessrestrict.p: Box 128 (Audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Imaginal Life LLC on the Sinclair Lewis Fund and the Adele Gutman Nathan Theatrical Collection Fund, 2021.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into seven series: I. Scripts and production materials, circa 1948-1987, undated. II. Correspondence, 1956 - 1987. III. Theater group records, 1955-1986. undated. IV. Photographs, 1952-1986, undated. V. Personal papers, 1836-1986, undated. VI. Printed materials, 1932-1986, undated. VII. Audiocassettes, 1978-1982, undated.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 128 (Audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The material in this series is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

did.unittitle: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences
Clothes for a summer hotel: negatives
Dulcy, a comedy in three acts: script
Sweet bird of youth June 1962: diary transcript

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The material in this series is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 137 (Computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 137 (Computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Imaginal Life LLC on the Adele Gutman Nathan Theatrical Collection Fund, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged into four groupings: I. Scripts and production materials, 1961 - 1984. II. Personal papers, 1924 - 1986. III. Printed material, 1953-1986. IV. Computer media, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains scripts, correspondence, printed material, photographs, realia, ephemera, and other materials related to the life and work of Geraldine Page.
